title: 'Abigail' Box Office Makes $1 Million in Thursday Previews
text: The vampire horror film Abigail generated approximately $1 million during Thursday night previews, according to a Variety report from April 19, 2024. The movie, directed by Radio Silence (Matt Bettinelli-Olpin and Tyler Gillett), is projected to earn between $12-15 million during its opening weekend. This figure would position it ahead of the previous week's box office leader, A24's Civil War. The R-rated Universal production carries a production budget of $28 million, meaning strong opening numbers suggest profitability throughout its theatrical release. Abigail stars Alisha Weir as the titular character—a young vampire ballerina and daughter of Count Dracula. The ensemble cast includes Melissa Barrera, Dan Stevens, Giancarlo Esposito, and others. The film reimagines the 1936 horror classic Dracula's Daughter based on a screenplay by Stephen Shields and Guy Busick. The plot follows criminals who kidnap the girl for a $50 million ransom scheme, only to discover they're trapped with a supernatural threat far more dangerous than anticipated.
